T0218.title: Refund Harvesting
T0218.definition: Purchase-and-return fraud is a scheme where fraudsters buy products in bulk and then return them en masse to exploit subsidies or rebates.
T0218.description: Operators typically take advantage of new-user promotions, discount subsidies, or commission policies on platforms. They place bulk orders and then trigger the return process to pocket the price difference or rebates. This activity is especially rampant during major e-commerce sales events, creating fake transactions that distort sales data and undermine platform risk control systems. The practice not only causes financial losses for merchants but may also lead to false flags against legitimate users.
T0218.usageExample: We can run purchase-and-return on this promotion—place orders to get the subsidies first, then return everything later.
